## Proposed Contributions

### commit/SKILL.md — new

- **Local path:** `.claude/skills/commit/SKILL.md`
- **Proposed target:** `shared/.claude/skills/commit/SKILL.md`
- **Type:** `new`
- **Description:** Generates well-formed git commit messages with prefix tags (feat/fix/refactor/test/docs/chore/style/perf/ci/revert). Warns when staged changes span unrelated concerns and prompts the user to split. Enforces one logical change per commit. User-invocable via `/commit`. Useful for any team wanting consistent commit hygiene regardless of stack.
- **Caveats:** None

### commit-best-practices.md — new

- **Local path:** `.claude/guides/commit-best-practices.md`
- **Proposed target:** `shared/.claude/guides/commit-best-practices.md`
- **Type:** `new`
- **Description:** Quick-reference guide for commit conventions: prefix tags, subject line rules (imperative mood, under 72 chars, no period), commit size rules, and grouping rules. Referenced directly by the commit skill. Should be shipped alongside `skills/commit/SKILL.md`.
- **Caveats:** None

## Notes for Reviewer

The commit skill consolidates the legacy `commit.md` command pattern. Going forward, `.claude/skills/commit/SKILL.md` should be the canonical approach — the skill format supports auto-triggering and richer frontmatter that commands do not. Any existing `commit.md` command file in bunzl-forge can be retired in favour of this skill.

The `forge-init.md` modification (removing a duplicate `mkdir docs`) was confirmed to already be present in the upstream forge repo — it is intentionally excluded from this contribution.
